page: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is an instance within the inf1 family, built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base speed of 2.5 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is not included on this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the inf1.2xlarge in the average tier for most standard compute and memory tasks. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench yield average results, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4565.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 8870.98. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 439.45 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 50.99 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In terms of specialized workloads, the instance achieves an average static web serving throughput of 6.88 GB/sec and handles 7.75 million Redis SET operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks show weaker performance, with the 7B model prompt processing dropping to 11.45 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inf1.2xlarge offers a dedicated compute environment with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance is suited for light to moderate web serving, Redis caching, software compilation, and small-scale machine learning inference tasks using models under 1B parameters, where prompt processing speeds remain average.
description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is a dedicated compute instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, with no local storage. Benchmarks indicate average performance across standard CPU and memory operations, including a PassMark CPU score of 8870.98 and a static web serving throughput of 6.88 GB/sec. While it handles small-scale LLM inference efficiently, performance drops significantly on larger 7B models. This instance is best suited for compute-focused workloads, software compilation, database caching, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers average performance for general compute, web serving, and small model inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering steady performance for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring balanced Intel Xeon processing and DDR4 memory for standard workloads and lightweight model inference.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Average performance in standard CPU, memory, and web serving benchmarks
Suitable for software compilation, Redis caching, and small-scale inference
